summary: The hexagonal split and Components V2 rendering are solid, but capture correctness can silently lose or miss operator feedback in multi-candidate and non-429 error paths.

issues.description: Reply pagination stops after the first matched reply across any candidate, which can miss older replies for other candidates in the same batch.
issues.explanation: This only stops pagination when every candidate target message has been observed, preventing false negatives where one candidate gets a `why` and others are missed because their replies are on older pages.

issues.description: `apiGetJson` swallows all non-429 failures into fallbacks, so auth/permission failures can be recorded as empty feedback instead of surfacing capture failure.
issues.explanation: Strict mode for required reads turns hard failures into explicit errors, preventing silent corpus poisoning from empty-but-invalid captures when token/permissions/network are broken.

blocking_issues.issue: Reply scan exits on first matched candidate instead of confirming coverage for all candidates.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Multi-candidate rounds can drop valid `why` feedback, reducing attribution quality and biasing preference records.
blocking_issues.fix: Track unresolved target message IDs and only break pagination once all are found (or page cap hit).

blocking_issues.issue: Non-429 Discord API failures are treated as empty data.
blocking_issues.why_blocking: Invalid auth/perms/network errors become silent empty captures, creating false "no feedback" records instead of actionable failures.
blocking_issues.fix: Add strict error mode for mandatory capture endpoints (`/users/@me`, message history, reactions) and throw on non-429 failures.
